project: A web application that lets freelancers log billable hours against client projects, set per-project rates and currencies, and export monthly PDF invoices summarising those hours.

slices.name: Sign in via OAuth on the web application
slices.value_basis: Authentication is the entry gate to every other capability; without it the product cannot be used at all. R1 defines the web delivery constraint that every other slice inherits.
slices.retires_risk: OAuth provider integration (A2): first exercise of the external OAuth delegation; proves the provider round-trip works before any other slice depends on an authenticated session.

slices.name: Log and persist billable time entries against a project
slices.value_basis: Logging hours is the core daily action the PROJECT describes; without it no invoice can be generated and the product has no primary value.

slices.name: Export a monthly PDF invoice with line items and monetary totals
slices.value_basis: PDF invoice export is the terminal deliverable named in the PROJECT statement; it is the output the freelancer sends to their client and the reason the product exists.
slices.retires_risk: Server-side PDF generation (A1): first exercise of the backend PDF library (e.g. Puppeteer or WeasyPrint); proves file generation and browser-stream delivery work before the feature is considered done.

slices.name: Create and manage client projects with currency and billable rate
slices.value_basis: Client projects are the structural container every other capability depends on; without a project no hours can be logged and no invoice can be generated. Multi-currency and rate configuration are set here and flow into every downstream slice.
